symptoms.symptom_name: Orthopnoea and paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea (three-pillow orthopnoea, waking gasping)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthopnoea and PND are specific for raised left-heart filling pressures and pulmonary congestion.
Positional, nocturnal fluid-redistribution dyspnoea is not typical of airways disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting hypoxia with increased work of breathing (SpO2 91% room air, accessory-muscle use)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoxia at rest indicates significant alveolar oedema and marks emergency-level severity; patient/carer-obtainable via oximeter so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive exertional dyspnoea with reduced exercise tolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Declining exercise tolerance reflects worsening cardiac reserve and congestion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly progressive bilateral peripheral oedema ascending to the thighs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dependent bilateral oedema is a hallmark of systemic venous congestion in right-sided/biventricular failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid weight gain (~5 kg over 48 hours)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid weight gain quantifies fluid retention — a sensitive marker of decompensation and diuretic deficit.
Missed loop diuretic is a classic, reversible precipitant of acute decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated jugular venous pressure (JVP ~8 cm above the sternal angle)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised JVP is a direct sign of elevated right-heart filling pressure; clinician-only, sealed.
An S3 gallop in this context is highly specific for raised filling pressures and systolic dysfunction; requires auscultation, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Displaced apex beat (to anterior axillary line) indicating cardiomegaly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lateral apex displacement suggests cardiomegaly / chronic ventricular remodelling; palpation-only, sealed.
Signs of systemic venous congestion; examination-only, sealed.
Crackles extending up the lung fields indicate alveolar pulmonary oedema; auscultation-only, sealed.
Crackles are non-specific, but bilateral dependent distribution with oedema favours cardiogenic oedema over consolidation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plasma BNP / NT-proBNP and basic metabolic panel (renal function, electrolytes)

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated natriuretic peptide consistent with cardiac decompensation; electrolytes/renal function to guide diuresis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without it, diuresis is titrated blind to renal/electrolyte status — a hospital, not a telehealth, investigation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A raised BNP supports cardiac cause of dyspnoea; lab-only, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no purulent sputum, no sudden pleuritic chest pain (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and purulent sputum makes infective consolidation less likely.
Gradual onset without sudden pleuritic pain makes acute PE less likely, though it cannot be excluded remotely.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Orthopnoea/PND + peripheral oedema + raised JVP + S3 gallop together are strongly diagnostic of decompensated congestive failure; the last two are clinician-only and sealed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms cardiac cause of acute dyspnoea and gauges severity — obtained in the emergency setting, not via telehealth.
Guides safe diuresis and detects hypokalaemia/renal impairment; a hospital investigation.
Confirms pulmonary oedema and helps exclude consolidation; in-person emergency investigation.
Defines underlying cardiac function once stabilised — the note flags formal echo is required; not a telehealth investigation.
Not indicated in acute decompensation with hypoxia; would waste time and is irrelevant to the emergency.
